body.purpose: Compact product chrome, tables, nav, controls, and summaries.

document.purpose: Markdown reader prose with comfortable line height.

mono.purpose: Paths, timestamps, generated metadata, IDs, and code.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Default hover and active state changes for buttons, nav items, links, and caret color.

motion.name: focus-standard
motion.purpose: Input border and background focus response.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Compact action button for IDE, folder, and source actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 6px 11px; border: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85)); border-radius: var(--r-sm, 4px); background: var(--bg, oklch(0.985 0.004 85)); color: var(--ink-70, oklch(0.40 0.008 80)); font-family: var(--font-sans, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 13px; transition: background 90ms ease-out, border-color 90ms ease-out, color 90ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--line-soft, oklch(0.95 0.005 85)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.21 0.008 80)); border-color: var(--ink-25, oklch(0.82 0.005 80)); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, oklch(0.52 0.08 245)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Active Navigation Item
components.description: Selected sidebar item with icon and label.
components.css: .ds-nav-active { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; width: 232px; padding: 7px 9px; border: 0; border-radius: var(--r-sm, 4px); background: var(--ink, oklch(0.21 0.008 80)); color: var(--bg, oklch(0.985 0.004 85)); font-family: var(--font-sans, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 14px; text-align: left; transition: background 90ms ease-out, color 90ms ease-out; } .ds-nav-active:hover { background: oklch(0.13 0.008 80); } .ds-nav-active:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, oklch(0.52 0.08 245)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 3px 9px 3px 7px; border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, var(--ok, oklch(0.62 0.10 155)) 30%, var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85))); border-radius: 999px; background: var(--ok-soft, oklch(0.96 0.03 155)); color: oklch(0.38 0.10 155); font-family: var(--font-sans, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-chip-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--ok, oklch(0.62 0.10 155)); }

components.name: Search Input
components.css: .ds-search-input { width: 320px; padding: 10px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85)); border-radius: var(--r-sm, 4px); background: var(--surface, oklch(0.995 0.003 85)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.21 0.008 80)); font-family: var(--font-sans, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif); font-size: 14px; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease-out, background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-search-input::placeholder { color: var(--ink-40, oklch(0.66 0.006 80)); } .ds-search-input:focus { border-color: var(--ink-25, oklch(0.82 0.005 80)); background: var(--bg, oklch(0.985 0.004 85)); }

components.name: Side Panel
components.description: Framed detail panel for workstream metadata and related tasks.
components.css: .ds-side-panel { width: 320px; border: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85)); border-radius: var(--r, 6px); background: var(--surface, oklch(0.995 0.003 85)); overflow: hidden; color: var(--ink, oklch(0.21 0.008 80)); font-family: var(--font-sans, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif); } .ds-side-head { padding: 12px 14px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85)); font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; } .ds-dl { margin: 0; padding: 8px 0; display: grid; grid-template-columns: max-content 1fr; gap: 0 16px; } .ds-dl dt { padding: 8px 14px; font-size: 12px; color: var(--ink-50, oklch(0.55 0.008 80)); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; font-weight: 500; } .ds-dl dd { margin: 0; padding: 8px 14px; font-size: 13px; } .ds-mono { font-family: var(--font-mono, ui-monospace, monospace); font-size: 12px; overflow-wrap: anywhere; }

components.name: Summary Strip
components.description: Ledger-style metadata row for status, owner, dates, and actions.
components.html:
Status
Complete
Owner
team
components.css: .ds-summary { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); border-top: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85)); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85)); font-family: var(--font-sans, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.21 0.008 80)); } .ds-field { padding: 16px 22px; border-right: 1px solid var(--line, oklch(0.92 0.005 85)); } .ds-field:first-child { padding-left: 0; } .ds-field:last-child { border-right: none; } .ds-label { margin-bottom: 6px; font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: var(--ink-40, oklch(0.66 0.006 80)); font-weight: 500; } .ds-value { font-size: 14px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; }

narrative.overview:
Delano Viewer should feel like a precise local dossier: quiet paper, clear indexes, restrained annotations, and no performance of importance. It is a localhost-only product UI where reading and navigating delivery contracts is the core job. The interface should disappear into the task, but never become vague.

The design language is document-like and deliberate. It uses warm near-white surfaces, near-black ink, hairline dividers, small status dots, and measured typography to make specs, plans, workstreams, tasks, and evidence easier to inspect. It rejects flashy, decorative, and marketing-led treatment. It also rejects generic SaaS dashboard tropes when they compete with the contracts themselves.
keyCharacteristics: Warm neutral paper surfaces with tinted ink, never pure black or pure white.
Restrained slate accent used only for selected state and workflow signal.
Flat hierarchy through borders, spacing, sticky navigation, and typography.
Read-only product posture: provenance and source paths stay visible.

rules.name: The Contract Ink Rule
rules.body: Text and source structure carry the interface. Color is evidence, state, or selection, never ornament.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Slate accent appears on less than 10% of a screen. If it is visible everywhere, it has lost its meaning.

rules.name: The Reading Surface Rule
rules.body: Document body text gets more line height than the product chrome. The UI is compact; the contract is comfortable.

rules.name: The Mono Evidence Rule
rules.body: Use monospace only where exact provenance matters. Do not use it as a decorative technology cue.

rules.name: The Flat Ledger Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are separated by line, tone, and position. Shadows are prohibited unless a future interactive overlay genuinely needs spatial separation.

rules.name: The Blur Is Utility Rule
rules.body: Backdrop blur belongs only to sticky chrome that must remain readable over moving content. It is not a glass effect.

dos: Do use OKLCH tokens from the viewer CSS as the canonical palette.
Do keep the default color strategy restrained: warm neutrals, one slate accent, and semantic status colors.
Do preserve source paths, timestamps, generated-at labels, and read-only cues as visible provenance.
Do separate dense information with hairline borders and varied spacing instead of stacked cards.
Do make contracts comfortable to read: 15px document body, 1.65 line height, and max prose width around 65 to 75 characters.
Do use labels and dots together for status.
donts: Don't make Delano flashy, decorative, or marketing-led.
Don't use generic SaaS dashboard tropes, hero metrics, promotional landing-page composition, or ornamental UI inside the product surface.
Don't use playful mascot-driven agent tooling, heavy enterprise PM-suite density, or dark cyber-terminal styling.
Don't add gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, colored side-stripe borders, or identical icon-card grids.
Don't turn every repeated item into a card. Tables, lists, and summary strips are first-class structures.
Don't use the slate accent as decoration. It is for selection and workflow signal only.
